evidence-verification-gate-for-synthesis.reason: This DR sets E.2g reverification-scope policy (full re-synthesis); it asserts no BPC synthesis claim and cites no source, so the rule #10 gate is not exercised here. It binds the future per-BPC E.2g runs.

best-practice-synthesis-routing.reason: Policy decision about whether prior synthesis prose may be salvaged; no best_practice_synthesis is written in this DR.

bias_direction: The 'full re-synthesis, discard prior prose' ruling conveniently lets the synthesizer avoid the harder cognitive task of critically auditing and editing forward 68 prior syntheses; 'anchor bias' can function as a rationalization for not engaging with prior work. It also enlarges the total work the synthesizer commits to, which could read as scope inflation. The honest counterweight: the prior text is already RETRACTED — formally deemed unreliable — so re-verifying it forward costs the same gate-work as fresh synthesis while adding anchoring risk; the claim-inventory carve-out preserves the genuine value (knowing what was asserted).
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: A reviewer would object that discarding 68 syntheses' prose wastes real prior labor and that selective salvage of the defensible parts would be more efficient. Counter: under rule #10 the drafting minutes are a small fraction of per-BPC cost (the re-reads and PMP walks dominate), and 'defensible parts' cannot be identified without the same verification that fresh synthesis requires — so salvage offers little time saving against material anchor-bias exposure in a single-reviewer setting.

reattestation.reason: Delta ratified the doctrine whose reverification this DR scopes. The E.2g reverification-scope decision (full re-synthesis) is unchanged by the later T4-6 rendering delta. Verdict CLEAN stands.
